perturbation.original_text: 2.9 Change Order. "Change Order" means any written request by any Party hereto to make any change to the Work, including the Services, Material, Delivery Dates or Completion Dates, Specifications or other terms and conditions of this Agreement, that is authorized and approved in writing by the other Party in accordance with the Change Management Plan.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.9 Change Order. "Change Order" means any written request by any Party hereto to make any change to the Work, including the Services, Material, Delivery Dates or Completion Dates, Specifications or other terms and conditions of this Agreement, that is authorized and approved in writing by the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "in accordance with the Change Management Plan," this change introduces an in-text contradiction. The contract still defines 'Change Order' but removes the requirement that the change be managed according to a specified plan. This contradicts Section 3.3(a) which still references the Change Management Plan. Section 3.3(a) states: If Vendor desires to request an extension of the applicable Delivery Date or Completion Date implicated by the Delay Information, Vendor may submit a change request to AT&T in accordance with the Change Management Plan.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Anticipated Delays. Upon discovery of information indicating that Material and/or Services will not be Delivered by the scheduled Delivery Date or Completion Date (including any scheduled Completion Date for a Milestone, scheduled Completion Date for Location Acceptance or scheduled Phase Completion Date set forth in the Build Addendum) or other information causing Vendor to reasonably anticipate a delay in its performance of its obligations beyond the scheduled Delivery Date or Completion Date (collectively, the "Delay Information"), Vendor shall promptly notify AT&T of such Delay Information and the estimated length of the anticipated delay. If Vendor desires to request an extension of the applicable Delivery Date or Completion Date implicated by the Delay Information, Vendor may submit a change request to AT&T in accordance with the Change Management Plan. The Parties shall cooperate and work jointly in good faith toward resolving the delayed Delivery or Completion or adopting the requested Change Order.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines a 'Change Order' as a written request approved in accordance with the Change Management Plan. The changed text removes this requirement. Section 3.3(a) still requires change requests to be submitted 'in accordance with the Change Management Plan.' By removing the reference to the Change Management Plan in the definition of 'Change Order,' the modified text directly contradicts Section 3.3(a), creating an in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition, because essential language was removed from the definition in one section, contradicting an obligation in another section. It is no longer clear whether all Change Orders are subject to the Change Management Plan.

perturbation.original_text: 3.8 Construction and Interpretation (a) This Agreement has been prepared jointly and has been the subject of arm's length and careful negotiation. Each Party has been given the opportunity to independently review this Agreement with legal counsel and other consultants, and each Party has the requisite experience and sophistication to understand, interpret and agree to the particular language of its provisions. Accordingly, the drafting of this Agreement is not to be attributed to either Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.8 Construction and Interpretation (a) This Agreement has been prepared jointly and has been the subject of arm's length and careful negotiation. Each Party has been given the opportunity to independently review this Agreement with legal counsel and other consultants, and each Party has been given the requisite experience and sophistication to understand, interpret and agree to the particular language of its provisions.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "Accordingly, the drafting of this Agreement is not to be attributed to either Party.", this change introduces an in-text contradiction. This clause prevents the contract from being construed against the drafter in case of ambiguity. If a court later interprets a clause ambiguously, it will be difficult to determine whose interpretation prevails, particularly given AT&T's power in the deal.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.8 Construction and Interpretation (a) This Agreement has been prepared jointly and has been the subject of arm's length and careful negotiation. Each Party has been given the opportunity to independently review this Agreement with legal counsel and other consultants, and each Party has been given the requisite experience and sophistication to understand, interpret and agree to the particular language of its provisions.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a clause preventing the contract from being construed against the drafter. By removing this clause in the changed text, it introduces ambiguity regarding whose interpretation prevails in case of a dispute. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this instance but existed elsewhere) now lacks this protection, creating a direct conflict because, without the clause, the agreement could be construed against a specific party, contradicting the intent that no party's interpretation should automatically prevail. This aligns with the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction, as essential language has been removed, creating uncertainty.

perturbation.original_text: 3.15 Indemnity (a) Except for Covered Losses that are exclusively provided for in the Section entitled "Infringement," Vendor shall indemnify, hold harmless, and defend the AT&T Indemnified Parties, in accordance with this Section, against any Third Party Loss arising from, or in connection with, or resulting from, (i) any default, violation, breach or nonperformance by Vendor or any Vendor Person of Vendor's representations, warranties, covenants and obligations under this Agreement, the Build Addendum or the Maintenance Addendum, (ii) the Deliverables, Material or Services furnished by Vendor or any Vendor Person and the use, construction, maintenance, operation or occupancy of any Cell Site by Vendor or any Vendor Person, (iii) the negligent or willful acts or omissions of Vendor and each Vendor Person with respect to this Agreement or (iv) Employment Claims. Subject to AT&T's obligation to reimburse or indemnify Vendor as set forth in Subsection (c) or (d) below, respectively, Vendor's duty to indemnify, hold harmless, and defend the AT&T Indemnified Parties against any Third Party Loss extends to any Third Party Loss that may be caused or alleged to be caused in part by the negligence of any of the AT&T Indemnified Parties to the fullest extent that such indemnification is permitted by applicable Law.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.15 Indemnity (a) Except for Covered Losses that are exclusively provided for in the Section entitled "Infringement," Vendor shall indemnify, hold harmless, and defend the AT&T Indemnified Parties, in accordance with this Section, against any Third Party Loss arising from, or in connection with, or resulting from, (i) any default, violation, breach or nonperformance by Vendor or any Vendor Person of Vendor's representations, warranties, covenants and obligations under this Agreement, the Build Addendum or the Maintenance Addendum, (ii) the Deliverables, Material or Services furnished by Vendor or any Vendor Person and the use, construction, maintenance, operation or occupancy of any Cell Site by Vendor or any Vendor Person, (iii) the negligent or willful acts or omissions of Vendor and each Vendor Person with respect to this Agreement or (iv) Employment Claims. Subject to AT&T's obligation to reimburse or indemnify Vendor as set forth in Subsection (c) or (d) below, respectively, Vendor's duty to indemnify, hold harmless, and defend the AT&T Indemnified Parties against any Third Party Loss extends to any Third Party Loss that may be caused or alleged to be caused in part by the negligence of any of the AT&T Indemnified Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "to the fullest extent that such indemnification is permitted by applicable Law.", this change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text clearly states that indemnification is only required to the extent permitted by law. Removing this phrase makes the scope of indemnification potentially unlimited, which may violate statutory limitations on indemnification, creating a contradiction with the implied requirement to comply with all applicable laws, as stipulated in other sections.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6 Compliance with Laws. Vendor shall comply with all Laws applicable to Vendor's performance under this Agreement, including all Work contemplated herein and all Material, Services and Deliverables provided hereunder, and AT&T's utilization of the Cell Sites. Vendor shall procure all approvals, bonds, certificates, insurance, inspections, licenses, and permits that such Laws require for the performance of this Agreement. Vendor shall create and maintain any necessary records and provide any certificate, affidavit or other information or documentation requested or as otherwise required by AT&T: (a) to show compliance by Vendor and its Subcontractors with Laws, (b) to comply or otherwise establish AT&T's compliance with Laws or (c) to allow AT&T to timely respond to any complaints, filings, or other proceedings.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the vendor's indemnity obligation to the extent permitted by applicable law. By removing this limitation, the changed text potentially requires the vendor to indemnify AT&T even if such indemnification violates the law. This creates a direct conflict with Section 3.6, which requires the vendor to comply with all applicable laws. The changed text imposes a potentially unlawful obligation on the vendor, thereby contradicting the requirement for legal compliance elsewhere in the contract.
